retrievedChunks.text: Photosynthesis converts sunlight into food using chlorophyll.
Plants absorb CO2 and water to produce glucose and oxygen.
The light-dependent reactions occur in the thylakoid membranes.
The Calvin cycle fixes carbon using ATP and NADPH.
Chloroplasts are the organelles where photosynthesis occurs.
Oxygen is released as a byproduct of light-dependent reactions.
Light intensity affects the rate of photosynthesis significantly.
C4 plants have a specialized photosynthesis pathway for hot climates.
CAM plants open stomata at night to conserve water during photosynthesis.
The Z-scheme describes electron transport in the light reactions.
Rubisco is the key enzyme responsible for carbon fixation.
Photosystem I and II collaborate in the light-dependent reactions.

finalAnswer: Photosynthesis converts sunlight, CO2 and water into glucose and oxygen using chlorophyll.
